iran_nuke.notes: Day 15 06:00 — FALLING STABLE. No new nuclear-specific signals. Hegseth claims Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei 'likely disfigured' from earlier strikes — leadership instability is a wildcard but doesn't directly map to nuclear escalation signals. Intense strikes on central Tehran reported (Al Jazeera live). Iran still channeling response through conventional means: Hormuz blockade, missile barrages, oil infrastructure threats. Nuclear track continues de-escalation despite conventional escalation. 14% (no change).

iran_conventional.notes: Day 15 11:04 — IMMINENT RISING. (1) FIVE US REFUELING TANKERS DESTROYED at Saudi Arabia's Prince Salman Air Base (WSJ/Times of India) — direct hit on US military logistics at Gulf state facility. (2) SMOKE RISING from UAE energy facility (AFP). Iran urges UAE residents to 'stay away from ports.' (3) BAHRAIN: intercepted 124 Iranian missiles and 203 drones since war began. (4) Iran defense ministry threatens 'more powerful ballistic and precision missiles.' (5) Two US B-1 bombers depart RAF Fairford (AFP). (6) Death toll updated to 1,800+ including 8 US service members, 175 students killed at Iranian elementary school (CFR). (7) 5,000 additional US troops deployed (Sky News). (8) US Embassy Baghdad still smoldering after Kataib Hezbollah strike. Oil 40%+ above pre-war. Capped at 100.

israel_lebanon.notes: Day 15 06:04 — IMMINENT RISING. (1) Axios: Israel agreed to spare Beirut airport but NOT other infrastructure. (2) IDF bombed bridge in south Lebanon — claimed Hezbollah using for troop/weapon movement. (3) Ground invasion planning continues: seize area south of Litani, 'largest since 2006.' 'We are going to do what we did in Gaza.' (4) 773+ killed including 100+ children. 800K-1M displaced. (5) 2 IDF soldiers killed, 14 wounded. (6) Hezbollah Radwan forces attempting raids on northern Israel. UNIFIL: 210+ rockets since March 2. Capped at 100.

turkey.notes: CRITICAL RISING. Third intercept confirmed March 13. NATO spokesperson Allison Hart: 'NATO remains vigilant and stands firm in its defence of all allies.' Sirens blared in Adana (Incirlik province). Turkey formally asked Tehran to clarify. Iran denies targeting, calls incidents 'possible false flag.' Politico EU: one more intercept likely triggers Article 4 consultations. 53 + 6 (Iran coupling) = 59. One intercept from IMMINENT.

india.notes: ELEVATED STABLE. No new direct signals. Modi expressed 'deep concern,' spoke with Iranian President about Indian citizen safety. India condemns Pakistan strikes on Afghanistan, supports Afghan sovereignty — but this is diplomatic, not military escalation. UK FCDO advises against travel within 10km of India-Pakistan border. Pakistan-Afghanistan war now at IMMINENT (71) but no coupling rule FROM Pakistan to India. 20 (stable).

russia.notes: CRITICAL RISING. (1) ISW: Global oil price shock from Iran war has 'likely invalidated' assumptions about Russian economic downturn forcing Kremlin concessions — sanctions pressure weakened. (2) Russia condemns Ukrainian strike on Bryansk as 'terrorist attack,' accuses Britain of overstepping legal norms (Reuters). (3) Putin and Trump discussed both Iran and Ukraine wars — no breakthrough. (4) Russia 1,277,620 total casualties since 2022, 860 killed/wounded in last 24h. (5) Sweden boards suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker. 30 + 4 (Iran coupling) + 4 (Turkey coupling) = 38.

china.notes: DETERRENT STABLE. (1) CNBC/AP: China warplane flights near Taiwan plunged from 92 to 7 over past two weeks — sharpest drop in years, puzzling analysts. Suggests deliberate de-escalation or strategic patience while US overextends in ME. (2) US THAAD fully relocated from South Korea — all 6 launcher vehicles departed. Creates significant Asia defense gap. (3) PRC increased 2026 military budget and escalated rhetoric at NPC (March 12). (4) $14B Taiwan arms deal awaits Trump approval ahead of Xi summit March 31. China appears to be letting US overextend while quietly preparing. 12 + 6 (THAAD) - 3 (reduced flights) = 15.

north_korea.notes: CRITICAL RISING - MAJOR ESCALATION. (1) North Korea fired ~10 ballistic missiles in salvo Saturday morning (March 14) - not a single test but coordinated barrage from western coast. Japan confirms fell into Sea of Japan, traveled ~340km outside Japan EEZ. THIRD provocation this week. (2) Timed during US-South Korea joint military drills - direct provocation. (3) Launches came AFTER Trump's dialogue outreach - rejecting diplomacy. (4) South Korea JCS: 'unprecedented pace of provocations.' Escalation from routine testing to multi-missile salvo demonstrates coordination and capacity. 8 + 12 (missile test) + 10 (salvo/barrage) + 5 (rhetoric) + 2 (China coupling) = 37. CRITICAL.

russia_ukraine.notes: CRITICAL RISING. (1) Ceasefire by end of March at 2% on Polymarket — effectively dead. (2) ISW: Oil price shock invalidates assumptions about Russian economic pressure — war economy benefits from $100+ oil. (3) Russia condemns Bryansk strike as 'terrorist attack' using British missiles. (4) Ukrainian GUR strikes Russian radar stations and drone infrastructure. (5) France pledges continued support: 'Russia is mistaken, hoping for a respite.' 35 + 5 (Russia critical) + 5 (Turkey critical) = 45.

pakistan_afghanistan.notes: IMMINENT RISING — MAJOR ESCALATION. (1) PAKISTAN DECLARES 'OPEN WAR': Defence Minister Khawaja Asif announced state of 'open war' with Afghanistan. Pakistan escalated airstrikes across Afghanistan including Kabul and Kandahar. (2) AFGHANISTAN HITS ISLAMABAD: Afghan drone struck 'Hamza' military center in Islamabad's Faizabad area — first strike on Pakistan's capital. Pakistan claims drones intercepted. (3) KOHAT FORT DESTROYED: Taliban claims destruction of Kohat military fortress and Durand Line command center. (4) CIVILIAN CASUALTIES: Pakistan airstrikes kill civilians in Kabul (4) and Kandahar (2), destroy Kam Air fuel depot. (5) Taliban retaliates with cross-border drone strikes. (6) Nuclear dimension: A nuclear-armed state in open war with neighbor striking near capital = extreme escalation risk. 10 + 10 + 8 + 7 + 5 + 15 = 55 + 16 (nuclear_escalation signal) = 71. IMMINENT.

top_signals: FIVE US refueling tankers destroyed at Saudi Arabia's Prince Salman Air Base — direct hit on US military logistics at Gulf state soil. Massive escalation (WSJ/Times of India)
Smoke rising from UAE energy facility — Iran strikes Gulf state infrastructure. Iran warns UAE residents 'stay away from ports' (AFP)
Bahrain intercepted 124 Iranian missiles and 203 drones since war began — Gulf state under sustained attack (Times of India)
Iran defense ministry threatens 'more powerful ballistic and precision missiles' — signals escalation from current arsenal (Reuters)
Two US B-1 bombers depart RAF Fairford — UK bases activated for ME operations (AFP)
Iran death toll reaches 1,800+ including 8 US troops and 175 students killed at elementary school (CFR)
Pakistan President Zardari: Taliban 'crossed a red line' with drone strikes on Pakistani cities (The Hindu)
North Korea fires ~10 ballistic missiles in salvo during US-South Korea drills — THIRD provocation this week (Reuters/Bloomberg)

changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: ESCALATION — Day 15 11:04 SCAN. Global war probability 67% (IMMINENT). FOUR TRACKERS IMMINENT: Iran conventional (100), Israel-Lebanon (100), Pakistan-Afghanistan (71). Turkey critical (65) — one intercept from Article 4. MAJOR NEW: (1) US military tankers destroyed at SAUDI ARABIA — Gulf state soil now active combat zone for US forces. (2) UAE energy facility struck — second Gulf state infrastructure hit. (3) Bahrain intercepted 124+203 Iranian projectiles. (4) Iran escalates rhetoric to 'more powerful missiles.' (5) B-1 bombers deploy from UK. NO de-escalation signals in any theater. All trajectories rising or stable-high.

latest_news.headline: Five US refueling tankers destroyed at Saudi Arabia's Prince Salman Air Base
latest_news.text: Five US military refueling aircraft were struck and destroyed on the ground at Prince Salman Air Base in Saudi Arabia. Wall Street Journal confirmed the attack. Represents direct Iranian strike on US military assets stationed at a Gulf state — dramatically expands the theater of direct US-Iran combat to Saudi soil.

latest_news.headline: Smoke rising from UAE energy facility — Iran warns UAE residents to 'stay away from ports'
latest_news.text: Smoke seen rising from direction of UAE energy facility (AFP). Iran military simultaneously urges all UAE residents to 'stay away from ports.' Second confirmed Gulf state infrastructure strike this week after Dubai. Iran escalating from threats to direct action against Gulf state assets linked to US interests.

latest_news.headline: Bahrain says it intercepted 124 Iranian missiles and 203 drones since war began
latest_news.text: Bahrain's defense ministry confirmed intercepting 124 Iranian missiles and 203 drones since the US-Israel war on Iran began Feb 28. Small Gulf state under sustained bombardment. Home to US Navy Fifth Fleet HQ.

latest_news.headline: Iran defense ministry threatens 'more powerful ballistic and precision missiles'
latest_news.text: Iran's defense ministry spokesperson warned the country will deploy 'ballistic missiles and other types of weapons with greater destructive power and precision' in ongoing operations. Signals escalation from current missile arsenal to heavier systems.

latest_news.headline: Two US B-1 bombers take off from UK's RAF Fairford
latest_news.text: Two US B-1 Lancer bombers departed RAF Fairford in UK, AFP reports. UK bases now actively supporting ME operations. B-1s capable of carrying massive conventional payloads. Signals US force buildup continuing despite 4-front overextension.

latest_news.headline: Pakistan President Zardari: Taliban 'crossed a red line' with drone strikes on Pakistani cities
latest_news.text: Pakistan President Asif Ali Zardari declared Afghan Taliban 'crossed a red line' by launching drone attacks on civilian targets in Pakistan. Military says 'rudimentary drones' targeted Abbottabad, Swabi, Nowshera — all foiled. Reinforces open war status.

latest_news.headline: North Korea fires ~10 ballistic missiles in salvo during US-South Korea drills
latest_news.text: North Korea fired approximately 10 ballistic missiles from its western coast Saturday morning. Japan confirms projectiles traveled ~340km into Sea of Japan outside Japan EEZ. Coordinated salvo during US-South Korea joint drills. Third provocation this week. South Korea JCS calls it unprecedented pace.

latest_news.headline: Global death toll from Iran war exceeds 1,800 including 8 US troops and 175 students at elementary school
latest_news.text: Council on Foreign Relations updates Iran war death toll to 1,800+ killed, including 8 US service members and at least 175 students killed by a reported US strike on an Iranian elementary school. 18,551+ injured. Humanitarian catastrophe deepening.

latest_news.headline: NATO intercepts third Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey — one from Article 4
latest_news.text: NATO air defenses intercepted third Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey in 10 days. Sirens in Adana near Incirlik Air Base. Turkey asked Tehran to clarify. Iran denies targeting. Politico EU: one more intercept likely triggers Article 4 consultations.

latest_news.headline: China warplane flights near Taiwan plunge from 92 to 7 in two weeks — analysts puzzled
latest_news.text: China's regular warplane flights near Taiwan dropped sharply from 92 to just 7 over the past two weeks. Analysts unsure if deliberate de-escalation or strategic patience while US overextends in ME. ISW notes PRC increased 2026 military budget simultaneously.

outlook: CRITICAL IMMINENT — Global war probability 67%. FOUR TRACKERS IMMINENT (Iran 100, Israel-Lebanon 100, Pakistan-Afghanistan 71) + Turkey at 65 (near-imminent). ESCALATION ACCELERATING: (1) US MILITARY TANKERS DESTROYED at Saudi Prince Salman Air Base — Gulf state soil now active combat zone for US forces. (2) UAE energy facility STRUCK — second Gulf state infrastructure hit after Dubai. Iran warns residents away from ports. (3) Bahrain intercepted 124 missiles + 203 drones — sustained Gulf bombardment confirmed. (4) Iran threatens 'more powerful ballistic and precision missiles' — escalation to heavier arsenal. (5) Two B-1 bombers deploy from RAF Fairford. (6) Death toll 1,800+ incl. 8 US troops, 175 schoolchildren. (7) China de-escalation: flights near Taiwan at historic low (7 in 2 weeks). STRATEGIC PICTURE: US now effectively fighting on 4 fronts (Iran, Lebanon, Pakistan-Afghanistan spillover, DPRK provocations) while China exercises strategic patience. Oil super-spike benefits Russia. No de-escalation signals in any theater. WATCH: (a) Iran escalation to heavier missiles. (b) Gulf state infrastructure campaign expansion. (c) 4th Turkey intercept → Article 4. (d) Lebanon ground invasion. (e) DPRK follow-up. (f) US military overextension breaking point. (g) China quiet military buildup while US overextends.
